chief_complaint: An 80-year-old woman presented with melena 2 years after surgery for small cell lung carcinoma.
history: The patient had a history of lobectomy for small cell lung carcinoma, presented with melena 2 years after surgery, and underwent contrast-enhanced abdominal CT and enteroscopy. She also had a history of ongoing hemodialysis.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Contrast-enhanced abdominal CT and enteroscopy revealed a bleeding tumor at the proximal jejunum. Histopathology confirmed jejunal metastasis from small cell lung carcinoma.
correct_diagnosis: Jejunal metastasis from small cell lung carcinoma
differentials: Jejunal metastasis from small cell lung carcinoma
correct_ranking: Jejunal metastasis from small cell lung carcinoma
